tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Key tests like test_standard_schema_validation and test_validate_partial_row_exported actually execute TypeScript code that imports modules, creates tables, calls functions, and checks runtime behavior. While some tests (test_write_path_uses_shared_validator, test_timestamp_schema_uses_create_schema) are text-based, the majority of behavioral tests execute real code. The test suite also runs typecheck, lint, format, and existing unit tests.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_standard_schema_validation creates a table and verifies that parseSafe works with it, checking that partial inputs are accepted, unknown columns are rejected, and invalid values are rejected. A stub returning defaults would fail these checks. test_validate_partial_row_exported calls the function with valid and invalid inputs and checks different return shapes. These require real implementation.
